iterations.strategy: Root cause is plan/implementation mismatch: Iter 0 still pays full-table fact scan and per-row hash aggregation despite available posting indexes, so main_scan dominates. Strategy Category A/C: reorder to filter sub(fy=2022) first and drive fact access through num_adsh_fk_hash postings, apply USD via a compact rowid bitset built from num_uom_hash, and replace per-row hash updates with thread-local direct arrays over filtered sub keys before a small final rollup; this removes unnecessary row touches and hot-path hash work while preserving query semantics.

Root cause is that iter_0 still performs too much per-row hot-path work: a full 39.4M-row fact loop plus two hash-map updates for each qualifying row. Strategy Category C (work elimination) with cache-fit array aggregation: drive scan from the USD posting list so only USD rowids are touched, map adsh codes through a dense filtered-index array, accumulate into thread-local fixed arrays keyed by filtered sub rows, and defer hash aggregation to a small post-scan rollup over filtered rows; this removes one entire per-row aggregation path and replaces random hash updates with contiguous array writes while preserving exact query semantics.

Root cause is wasted work in the previous direction: build_joins became dominant by materializing USD rowids from a cache-exceeding posting tail before any selective join. Strategy Category A/C: reorder to filter sub by fy first, then use the existing num.adsh.triple_ranges representation to scan only fact ranges for matching adsh keys and apply uom/value predicates inside those ranges; keep aggregation as cache-friendly direct arrays with a small final rollup for HAVING and top-k.

Root cause is logical/physical overwork in fact access: the current plan uses num.adsh.triple_ranges over a tag-version posting structure even though Q3 joins only on adsh, adding avoidable per-entry indirection inside the hot scan. Strategy Category A/C: restructure join probing to use num_adsh_fk_hash directly from filtered sub(adsh, fy=2022), scan only those posting rowids, and keep per-thread direct-array aggregation with a small final rollup for HAVING and top-k.

Root cause is a physical access-pattern mismatch: probing filtered adsh posting lists drives cache-miss-heavy random reads into num.value/num.uom (working set >> 88MB LLC), so main_scan remains dominant. Strategy Category B/A: replace posting-driven probes with one fused sequential fact pass over num.adsh/uom/value, use a dense adsh->filtered_sub_idx direct lookup from filtered sub(fy=2022), and keep cache-friendly thread-local direct-array aggregation before a small final rollup for HAVING and top-k.
